cover_caption: OmniRetarget flagship demo: a Unitree G1 humanoid carries a chair, climbs a platform, leaps down, and rolls in a 30-second long-horizon loco-manipulation sequence. Video source: omniretarget.github.io
source_title: OmniRetarget: Interaction-Preserving Data Generation for Humanoid Whole-Body Loco-Manipulation and Scene Interaction

figures.caption: OmniRetarget overview. Human demonstrations are retargeted to the robot via interaction-mesh-based constrained optimization. Each spatial and shape augmentation is solved as a new optimization, producing diverse trajectories that serve as references for RL training with minimal reward design and domain randomization, enabling zero-shot transfer to real-world humanoids.
figures.caption_ko: OmniRetarget 개요. 인간 시연을 interaction mesh 기반 constrained optimization으로 로봇에 retarget하고, 공간/형상 augmentation마다 새 최적화를 풀어 다양한 RL reference trajectory를 만든다. 이 데이터는 최소한의 reward design과 domain randomization만으로 실제 휴머노이드 zero-shot transfer를 가능하게 한다.

figures.caption: OmniRetarget generates systematic variations of (a) terrain height, (b) object initial pose, and (c) object shape from a single human demonstration, with optimized motions in simulation (top) transferring consistently to hardware (bottom).
figures.caption_ko: OmniRetarget은 하나의 인간 시연에서 terrain height, object initial pose, object shape 변형을 체계적으로 만들고, 시뮬레이션에서 최적화한 motion이 실제 하드웨어로 일관되게 전이되는 것을 보여준다.

figures.caption: Additional hardware results showing diverse, agile and human-like behaviors.
figures.caption_ko: 다양하고 민첩하며 인간적인 동작을 보여주는 추가 하드웨어 결과.

figures.caption: Hardware results showing a high-dynamic wall-flip motion. The robot reaches a maximum linear velocity of 3.5 m/s and a peak angular velocity of 15 rad/s.
figures.caption_ko: 고동적 wall-flip motion의 하드웨어 결과. 로봇은 최대 선속도 3.5 m/s와 peak angular velocity 15 rad/s에 도달한다.

figures.caption: Artifacts resulting from the retargeting baselines.

tables.caption: TABLE II: Quantitative comparison of kinematic retargeting quality and downstream RL performances.
tables.caption_ko: TABLE II: kinematic retargeting 품질과 downstream RL 성능의 정량 비교.

ai_summary.one_liner: OmniRetarget turns a single human whole-body demonstration into interaction-preserving humanoid reference trajectories by optimizing an interaction mesh with hard physical constraints.
ai_summary.problem: Humanoid whole-body loco-manipulation needs human-like references, but common retargeting pipelines lose object/terrain contact structure and produce penetration or foot-skating artifacts that make downstream RL brittle.
ai_summary.solution: Build an interaction mesh over body, object, and terrain points, minimize Laplacian deformation while enforcing collision, joint, velocity, and foot-contact constraints, then re-solve the optimization under object, terrain, and embodiment augmentations.
ai_summary.key_result: The method generates 8+ hours of retargeted trajectories and enables Unitree G1 policies to execute up to 30-second parkour/loco-manipulation skills with only five reward terms and simple domain randomization; Table II reports 82.20% robot-object and 94.73% robot-terrain downstream RL success.
limitations: The current frame-by-frame optimization is effective but future work may need joint trajectory optimization for noisier motion sources such as video.
Some extremely difficult motions still expose limits of the simple RL formulation, motivating curriculum learning or richer policy training.
The paper reports strong Unitree G1 results, but cross-lab reproducibility depends on access to comparable mocap, simulation, robot, and evaluation infrastructure.

research_gaps.question: Can interaction-preserving retargeting remain stable when the source motion is extracted from noisy monocular video rather than clean motion capture?
How should whole-trajectory optimization and RL curriculum be coupled for the remaining high-difficulty agile motions?
Can the interaction mesh abstraction generalize from rigid object/terrain contacts to deformable, multi-object, or tactile-rich manipulation?

future_directions: Jointly optimize entire trajectories instead of solving only frame by frame, especially for noisy video-derived sources.
Combine interaction-preserving retargeting with curriculum or residual RL for motions that are too difficult for the current minimal policy formulation.
Standardize open benchmarks for humanoid object-terrain interaction so retargeting quality and downstream policy robustness can be compared across labs.
Extend the interaction mesh to richer contact modalities, including deformable objects and tactile feedback.
ai_research_question: What changes when humanoid robot learning treats interaction-preserving retargeted data as the core bottleneck, instead of trying to patch poor references with more reward engineering?
